name: wp-plugin-development
description: Use when developing WordPress plugins: architecture and hooks, activation/deactivation/uninstall, admin UI and Settings API, data storage, cron/tasks, security (nonces/capabilities/sanitization/escaping), and release packaging.
capabilities: Create and refactor WordPress plugin structure with proper architecture
Implement activation, deactivation, and uninstall hooks with best practices
Build admin settings pages using WordPress Settings API
Apply security measures including nonces, capabilities, sanitization, and escaping
Manage data storage options and cron tasks in plugins
Package plugins for release with proper build artifacts and readme files
author: Automattic
securitySummary: This skill is documentation-only guidance for WordPress plugin development. Static findings are false positives from scanner misidentifying WordPress PHP API functions (e.g., register_activation_hook, $wpdb->prepare) as shell commands or crypto. The only code is a safely bounded Node.js file scanner (max 6000 files, ignores node_modules/.git). No network calls, credential access, or dangerous operations.
valueStatement: Creating WordPress plugins requires understanding complex hooks, lifecycle management, and security practices. This skill provides expert guidance for plugin architecture, Settings API integration, and secure development patterns.
limitations: Does not execute PHP code or install plugins on a live WordPress site
Does not provide hosting or deployment services
Does not modify database schema directly without guidance

useCases.title: Create New Plugins
useCases.description: Set up proper plugin structure, hooks, and lifecycle management for new WordPress extensions.

useCases.title: Add Settings Pages
useCases.description: Build admin interfaces using Settings API with proper sanitization and capability checks.

useCases.title: Fix Security Issues
useCases.description: Implement nonces, escape output, use prepared statements, and apply capability verification.

promptTemplates.title: New Plugin Structure
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a new WordPress plugin with proper structure. Include a bootstrap file with plugin header, a loader class for hooks, and activation/deactivation handlers.

promptTemplates.title: Settings Page
promptTemplates.prompt: Add a settings page to my WordPress plugin using the Settings API. Include register_setting, add_settings_section, and proper capability checks.

promptTemplates.title: Security Audit
promptTemplates.prompt: Review my WordPress plugin for security issues. Check for proper nonce validation, capability checks, input sanitization, and output escaping.

promptTemplates.title: Release Package
promptTemplates.prompt: Prepare my WordPress plugin for release on wordpress.org. Include proper readme.txt, build assets, and verify all security requirements are met.

bestPractices: Always validate and sanitize input early, escape output late
Use nonces to prevent CSRF attacks combined with capability checks for authorization
Store schema version and provide upgrade paths when modifying database tables
antiPatterns: Avoid processing entire $_POST or $_GET arrays; read explicit keys only
Never concatenate user input directly into SQL queries; use $wpdb->prepare()
Do not register activation hooks inside other hooks or conditional blocks

faq.question: What WordPress versions does this skill support?
faq.answer: Targets WordPress 6.9+ with PHP 7.2.24+. Some features require WP-CLI for testing.

faq.question: What are the limits on plugin complexity?
faq.answer: The skill handles single-site and multisite setups. Complex plugins may need additional architectural guidance.

faq.question: How does this integrate with existing tools?
faq.answer: Works with WP-CLI for command-line operations. Provides guidance for integrating with build tools and CI/CD.

faq.question: Is my data safe when using this skill?
faq.answer: This is a guidance-only skill. No code execution or file modifications happen without explicit user direction.

faq.question: Why is my settings page not saving?
faq.answer: Check that register_setting is called, option group matches form, capability and nonce are verified, and sanitize_callback is defined.

faq.question: How does this compare to other development skills?
faq.answer: This skill focuses specifically on WordPress plugin patterns. General PHP skills cover syntax; this covers WordPress APIs and conventions.
